question: [File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/TransactionPublisher.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: processTransactionResponse()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a scenario where neither `request.isSendRecord()` nor `request.isReceipt()` is true, causing `processTransactionResponse()` to return `Mono.just(builder)` with no record or receipt attached, resulting in the mirror node receiving a publish response with missing transaction record data?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/TransactionPublisher.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: publish()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a `PublishRequest` with a `timeout` set to an extremely short `Duration` via `PublishScenarioProperties`, causing `publish()` to emit a `TimeoutException` before `processTransactionResponse()` completes, resulting in the transaction record never being exported to the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/TransactionPublisher.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: toClient()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `(a, b) -> a` merge function in `toClient()` when multiple `NodeProperties` share the same endpoint, causing one node's `AccountId` to be silently dropped from the network map, resulting in transactions being routed to the wrong node and their records never appearing on the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/TransactionPublisher.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getClients()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that `clients` is a `Flux.defer(this::getClients).cache()` that is never invalidated, causing stale node configurations to persist indefinitely and transactions to be sent to decommissioned nodes whose records are never exported to the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/TransactionPublisher.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: processTransactionResponse()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a condition where `TransactionRecordQuery` with a fixed `Hbar.from(1, HbarUnit.HBAR)` payment fails due to insufficient query payment, causing the record fetch to throw an exception that is wrapped by `onErrorMap()` into a `PublishException`, resulting in the transaction record being missing from the mirror node export?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/TransactionPublisher.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: publish()] Can an unprivileged external user cause `clients.elementAt(clientIndex)` to emit an `IndexOutOfBoundsException` when `secureRandom.nextInt(publishProperties.getClients())` returns an index beyond the actual number of initialized clients, resulting in the publish flow terminating without exporting any record to the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/TransactionPublisher.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getTransactionResponse()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the mutable `request.setNode(node)` assignment inside `getTransactionResponse()` in a concurrent reactive pipeline, causing a race condition where two concurrent publishes share the same `PublishRequest` object and one overwrites the other's node assignment, leading to transactions being sent to the wrong node and records being missing from the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/TransactionPublisher.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: processTransactionResponse()] Can an unprivileged external user cause `processTransactionResponse()` to use `Instant.now()` as the response timestamp instead of the actual consensus timestamp from the network, resulting in the `PublishResponse` carrying an incorrect timestamp that causes the mirror node subscriber to fail to match the record to the correct transaction?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/TransactionPublisher.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: publish()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `onErrorMap(t -> !(t instanceof PublishException), t -> new PublishException(request, t))` handler in `publish()` to silently swallow record-fetch errors by ensuring they are wrapped into `PublishException` and then consumed by `scenario::onError`, causing the mirror node to never receive the transaction record?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/TransactionPublisher.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: toClient()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `@SneakyThrows` annotation on `toClient()` to cause a checked exception (e.g., from `Client.forNetwork()` or `setNetworkFromAddressBook()`) to propagate as an unchecked exception that bypasses the reactive error handling chain, causing the client initialization to fail silently and all subsequent transactions to have missing records on the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/TransactionPublisher.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getClients()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a scenario where `nodeSupplier.refresh()` returns an empty list, causing `getClients()` to produce clients with an empty network map via `Client.forNetwork(Map.of())`, resulting in all transactions failing to reach any node and their records being absent from the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/TransactionPublisher.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: publish()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `recordPercent` and `receiptPercent` sampling in `PublishScenarioProperties` being set to `0.0` by default, causing `publish()` to never set `sendRecord` or `receipt` flags on `PublishRequest`, resulting in no records being exported to the mirror node for any published transaction?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/TransactionPublisher.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: execute()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the async/sync toggle in `execute()` via `publishProperties.isAsync()`, where switching between `Mono.fromFuture(executable.executeAsync(client))` and `Mono.fromCallable(() -> executable.execute(client))` mid-operation causes a thread-safety issue that results in incomplete or missing transaction records being exported to the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/TransactionPublisher.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: processTransactionResponse()] Can an unprivileged external user cause `TransactionReceiptQuery` in `processTransactionResponse()` to return a receipt with a non-SUCCESS status that is not validated before being stored in `PublishResponse`, resulting in the mirror node subscriber treating a failed transaction as successful and exporting incorrect records?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/TransactionPublisher.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: toClient()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `client.setNetwork(network)` call after `client.setNetworkFromAddressBook()` in `toClient()`, where the second call overwrites the address book configuration with a potentially incomplete network map, causing transactions to be routed incorrectly and their records to be missing from the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/TransactionPublisher.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: close()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger `close()` to silently swallow all `client.close()` exceptions via the empty `catch (Exception e) {}` block, causing clients to remain in a partially closed state where subsequent transactions are submitted but their records are never exported to the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/TransactionPublisher.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: publish()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that `publish()` returns `Mono.empty()` when `publishProperties.isEnabled()` is false, causing the calling pipeline in `MonitorConfiguration` to silently skip record export without any error signal, resulting in missing records on the mirror node with no observable failure?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/TransactionPublisher.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getTransactionResponse()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `transaction.setNodeAccountIds(node.getAccountIds())` mutation in `getTransactionResponse()` on a shared `Transaction<?>` object, causing concurrent publish calls to overwrite each other's node account IDs, resulting in transactions being submitted to incorrect nodes and their records being absent from the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/TransactionPublisher.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: processTransactionResponse()] Can an unprivileged external user cause `processTransactionResponse()` to use `transactionResponse.transactionId` directly without validating it is non-null, and if the SDK returns a null `transactionId` on certain error paths, the `TransactionRecordQuery.setTransactionId(null)` call silently produces an incorrect query, resulting in the wrong or missing record being exported to the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/TransactionPublisher.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: toClient()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `nodeAddresses` filtering in `toClient()` where `nodes.stream().filter(n -> accountIds.contains(n.getAccountId()))` silently drops nodes whose `accountId` is not in the deduplicated `network` map, causing those nodes to be excluded from the address book and their transactions to never be routed correctly, resulting in missing records on the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/TransactionPublisher.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: publish()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `secureRandom.nextInt(publishProperties.getClients())` call in `publish()` where `publishProperties.getClients()` returns a value larger than the actual number of cached clients, causing `clients.elementAt(clientIndex)` to return an empty `Mono`, which then causes the entire publish chain to complete without emitting a record, resulting in missing records on the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/TransactionPublisher.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: processTransactionResponse()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fixed `Hbar.from(1, HbarUnit.HBAR)` query payment in `TransactionRecordQuery` being insufficient for high-fee network conditions, causing the record query to be rejected by the node with `INSUFFICIENT_TX_FEE`, resulting in the transaction record never being fetched and exported to the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/TransactionPublisher.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getClients()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `Flux.range(0, publishProperties.getClients()).flatMap(i -> Mono.defer(() -> Mono.just(toClient(nodes))))` pattern in `getClients()`, where all clients are built from the same stale `nodes` list snapshot, causing all clients to use outdated node configurations and route transactions to nodes that no longer export records to the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/TransactionPublisher.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: publish()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `doOnNext(scenario::onNext)` and `doOnError(scenario::onError)` side effects in `publish()` throwing an unchecked exception inside the scenario callback, causing the reactive chain to terminate abnormally and the `PublishResponse` (containing the record) to never be forwarded to the mirror node subscriber?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/TransactionPublisher.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: execute()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `Mono.fromCallable(() -> executable.execute(client))` path in the synchronous `execute()` overload, where a blocking call on a reactive scheduler thread causes thread starvation, resulting in pending record queries timing out and transaction records being missing from the mirror node export?
